mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher in the top half of RSAFS 3-month momentum than in the bottom half over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected corporate revenues and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and raises the discount rate applied to future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after triggered FEDFUNDS increases are not lower than non-triggered periods in at least 60% of walk-forward validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales rising while industrial production falls indicates demand exceeding current supply capacity, raising margin pressure and inflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive while INDPRO 3-month growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if this joint condition does not predict lower 20-day returns than periods with both RSAFS and INDPRO rising, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its recent trend signals weakening real activity and lower operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 12-month moving average after being above it
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO downside crosses have non-negative average 5-day forward returns or if hit rate for negative returns is below 52% in out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above their recent trend signals durable-goods demand, construction income, and credit transmission strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 6-month moving average after being below it
failure_condition: Reject if upward HOUST crosses fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered instances or underperform the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market healing, supporting consumption and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth is positive and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not outperform either single-series signal and the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration without a matching policy-rate response lowers real policy restraint and increases expected future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its 12-month growth rate while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-policy divergences have average 5-day forward returns above the unconditional average or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in validation samples.
